Bead adc-1l8w; ``--container`` mode (bead adc-4nd25).\n5\t *\n6\t * canvas.js is written so its render helpers (createTopicCard et al.) can be\n7\t * exercised without a browser — its module docstring promises exactly this\n8\t * file:\n9\t *\n10\t *     \"tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js loads this exact file under a minimal\n11\t *      DOM shim and asserts the rendered HTML, with no browser or network\n12\t *      required.\"\n13\t *\n14\t * This is that file. It provides a minimal DOM shim that implements precisely\n15\t * the surface the render helpers touch — document.createElement,\n16\t * document.createTextNode, appendChild, className, classList.add, dataset,\n17\t * textContent (→ escaped innerHTML), and innerHTML get/set — then loads the\n18\t * actual src/canvas/canvas.js and renders the card dicts the canvas really\n19\t * receives from GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics.\n20\t *\n21\t * Two modes:\n22\t *\n23\t *   1. DEFAULT (bead adc-1l8w) — render an array of card dicts through\n24\t *      createTopicCard(), the function loadTopics() calls per card. Drives the\n25\t *      escaping contract for the topic-card family.\n26\t *\n27\t *   2. ``--container`` (bead adc-4nd25) — render the shared container-decision\n28\t *      core buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description), which is what\n29\t *      loadTopics() actually calls: on a fresh session with ZERO cards it\n30\t *      returns the first-run welcome card (built from the registry project\n31\t *      list — no DB/result dependence); the moment any real card exists it\n32\t *      returns topic cards only (dropping the welcome card, never alongside\n33\t *      real cards). This is the headlessly-testable contract the bead's\n34\t *      acceptance criteria name: \"welcome card renders from a zero-card\n35\t *      session and is dropped on first result.\"\n36\t *\n37\t * The Python contract tests (tests/test_canvas_render.py and\n38\t * tests/e2e/test_canvas_welcome_card.py) feed it JSON and assert the rendered\n39\t * outerHTML, so a regression in the render contract is caught headlessly, in\n40\t * CI, with no browser.\n41\t *\n42\t * Usage:\n43\t *   node canvas_dom_runner.js '<json array of card dicts>'\n44\t *   echo '<json array>' | node canvas_dom_runner.js        # stdin fallback\n45\t *   node canvas_dom_runner.js --container '{\"cards\":[...],\"projects\":[...],\"description\":\"...\"}'\n46\t *   echo '{...}' | node canvas_dom_runner.js --container   # stdin fallback\n47\t *\n48\t * Stdout: a JSON array of { outerHTML, className, dataset } per rendered node.\n49\t */\n50\t\n51\t\"use strict\";\n52\t\n53\tconst fs = require(\"fs\");\n54\tconst path = require(\"path\");\n55\t\n56\t// --- minimal DOM shim -------------------------------------------------------\n57\t\n58\tfunction escapeText(s) {\n59\t    // Matches the browser: textContent→innerHTML escapes &, <, > (not quotes).\n60\t    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/</g, \"&lt;\").replace(/>/g, \"&gt;\");\n61\t}\n62\t\n63\tfunction escapeAttr(s) {\n64\t    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/\"/g, \"&quot;\");\n65\t}\n66\t\n67\t// A text node (document.createTextNode). \"\" : String(text);\n73\t        this.nodeType = 3;\n74\t    }\n75\t\n76\t    set textContent(v) {\n77\t        this._text = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n78\t    }\n79\t\n80\t    get textContent() {\n81\t        return this._text;\n82\t    }\n83\t\n84\t    get outerHTML() {\n85\t        return escapeText(this._text);\n86\t    }\n87\t\n88\t    get innerHTML() {\n89\t        return escapeText(this._text);\n90\t    }\n91\t}\n92\t\n93\tclass ShimNode {\n94\t    constructor(tag) {\n95\t        this._tag = tag;\n96\t        this._classes = [];\n97\t        this._dataset = {};\n98\t        this._textContent = \"\";\n99\t        this._innerHTML = \"\";\n100\t        this._children = []; // built via appendChild (el()/createWelcomeCard path)\n101\t        this.style = {}; // present so style.* assignments are no-ops, not crashes\n102\t    }\n103\t\n104\t    set className(v) {\n105\t        this._classes = String(v).split(/\\s+/).filter(Boolean);\n106\t    }\n107\t\n108\t    get className() {\n109\t        return this._classes.join(\" \");\n110\t    }\n111\t\n112\t    get classList() {\n113\t        const self = this;\n114\t        return {\n115\t            add(c) {\n116\t                if (!self._classes.includes(c)) self._classes.push(c);\n117\t            },\n118\t            contains(c) {\n119\t                return self._classes.includes(c);\n120\t            },\n121\t            toggle(c, force) {\n122\t                const on = force === undefined ? Bead adc-1l8w; ``--container`` mode (bead adc-4nd25).\n *\n * canvas.js is written so its render helpers (createTopicCard et al.) can be\n * exercised without a browser — its module docstring promises exactly this\n * file:\n *\n *     \"tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js loads this exact file under a minimal\n *      DOM shim and asserts the rendered HTML, with no browser or network\n *      required.\"\n *\n * This is that file. It provides a minimal DOM shim that implements precisely\n * the surface the render helpers touch — document.createElement,\n * document.createTextNode, appendChild, className, classList.add, dataset,\n * textContent (→ escaped innerHTML), and innerHTML get/set — then loads the\n * actual src/canvas/canvas.js and renders the card dicts the canvas really\n * receives from GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics.\n *\n * Two modes:\n *\n *   1. DEFAULT (bead adc-1l8w) — render an array of card dicts through\n *      createTopicCard(), the function loadTopics() calls per card. Drives the\n *      escaping contract for the topic-card family.\n *\n *   2. ``--container`` (bead adc-4nd25) — render the shared container-decision\n *      core buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description), which is what\n *      loadTopics() actually calls: on a fresh session with ZERO cards it\n *      returns the first-run welcome card (built from the registry project\n *      list — no DB/result dependence); the moment any real card exists it\n *      returns topic cards only (dropping the welcome card, never alongside\n *      real cards). This is the headlessly-testable contract the bead's\n *      acceptance criteria name: \"welcome card renders from a zero-card\n *      session and is dropped on first result.\"\n *\n * The Python contract tests (tests/test_canvas_render.py and\n * tests/e2e/test_canvas_welcome_card.py) feed it JSON and assert the rendered\n * outerHTML, so a regression in the render contract is caught headlessly, in\n * CI, with no browser.\n *\n * Usage:\n *   node canvas_dom_runner.js '<json array of card dicts>'\n *   echo '<json array>' | node canvas_dom_runner.js        # stdin fallback\n *   node canvas_dom_runner.js --container '{\"cards\":[...],\"projects\":[...],\"description\":\"...\"}'\n *   echo '{...}' | node canvas_dom_runner.js --container   # stdin fallback\n *\n * Stdout: a JSON array of { outerHTML, className, dataset } per rendered node.\n */\n\n\"use strict\";\n\nconst fs = require(\"fs\");\nconst path = require(\"path\");\n\n// --- minimal DOM shim -------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction escapeText(s) {\n    // Matches the browser: textContent→innerHTML escapes &, <, > (not quotes).\n    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/</g, \"&lt;\").replace(/>/g, \"&gt;\");\n}\n\nfunction escapeAttr(s) {\n    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/\"/g, \"&quot;\");\n}\n\n// A text node (document.createTextNode). Its serialized form is the escaped\n// text — the escaping contract's literal form, since el()/createTextNode never\n// splice raw markup.\nclass ShimText {\n    constructor(text) {\n        this._text = text == null ? \"\" : String(text);\n        this.nodeType = 3;\n    }\n\n    set textContent(v) {\n        this._text = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n    }\n\n    get textContent() {\n        return this._text;\n    }\n\n    get outerHTML() {\n        return escapeText(this._text);\n    }\n\n    get innerHTML() {\n        return escapeText(this._text);\n    }\n}\n\nclass ShimNode {\n    constructor(tag) {\n        this._tag = tag;\n        this._classes = [];\n        this._dataset = {};\n        this._textContent = \"\";\n        this._innerHTML = \"\";\n        this._children = []; // built via appendChild (el()/createWelcomeCard path)\n        this.style = {}; // present so style.* assignments are no-ops, not crashes\n    }\n\n    set className(v) {\n        this._classes = String(v).split(/\\s+/).filter(Boolean);\n    }\n\n    get className() {\n        return this._classes.join(\" \");\n    }\n\n    get classList() {\n        const self = this;\n        return {\n            add(c) {\n                if (!self._classes.includes(c)) self._classes.push(c);\n            },\n            contains(c) {\n                return self._classes.includes(c);\n            },\n            toggle(c, force) {\n                const on = force === undefined ? !self._classes.includes(c) : !!force;\n                if (on && !self._classes.includes(c)) self._classes.push(c);\n                if (!on) self._classes = self._classes.filter((x) => x !== c);\n                return on;\n            },\n        };\n    }\n\n    get dataset() {\n        return this._dataset;\n    }\n\n    appendChild(node) {\n        this._children.push(node);\n        return node;\n    }\n\n    set textContent(v) {\n        this._textContent = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n        // Setting textContent replaces all children with a single text node —\n        // mirrors the browser, and keeps escapeHtml()'s \"set textContent then\n        // read innerHTML\" pattern producing escaped text.\n        this._children = [];\n        this._innerHTML = escapeText(this._textContent);\n    }\n\n    get textContent() {\n        return this._textContent;\n    }\n\n    set innerHTML(v) {\n        this._innerHTML = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n        this._children = []; // innerHTML= replaces children, browser-faithful\n    }\n\n    get innerHTML() {\n        // Children built via appendChild serialize recursively; a directly-set\n        // innerHTML string (the createTopicCard path) is used otherwise.\n        if (this._children.length) {\n            return this._children.map((c) => c.outerHTML).join(\"\");\n        }\n        return this._innerHTML;\n    }\n\n    _datasetAttrs() {\n        // dataset.topicId → data-topic-id (camelCase → kebab-case w/ data- prefix).\n        const out = [];\n        for (const key of Object.keys(this._dataset)) {\n            const name = \"data-\" + key.replace(/([A-Z])/g, \"-$1\").toLowerCase();\n            out.push(`${name}=\"${escapeAttr(this._dataset[key])}\"`);\n        }\n        return out;\n    }\n\n    get outerHTML() {\n        const cls = this._classes.length ? ` class=\"${this._classes.join(\" \")}\"` : \"\";\n        const attrs = this._datasetAttrs();\n        const attrStr = attrs.length ? \" \" + attrs.join(\" \") : \"\";\n        return `<${this._tag}${cls}${attrStr}>${this.innerHTML}</${this._tag}>`;\n    }\n}\n\n// canvas.js references `document` as a free variable inside its functions, so\n// it must resolve via the global scope at call time.\nglobal.document = {\n    createElement(tag) {\n        return new ShimNode(tag);\n    },\n    createTextNode(text) {\n        return new ShimText(text);\n    },\n};\n\n// --- load the REAL canvas.js ------------------------------------------------\n\nconst canvasPath = path.resolve(__dirname, \"..\", \"..\", \"src\", \"canvas\", \"canvas.js\");\nconst canvas = require(canvasPath); // { createTopicCard, escapeHtml, formatStaleness, getStalenessLevel }\n\n// --- render driver ----------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Read the JSON payload for the active mode. argvPos is the index of the\n// positional JSON arg (after any --mode flag); stdin is the pipe-friendly\n// fallback. Returns the parsed value.\nfunction readPayload(argvPos) {\n    if (process.argv[argvPos] !== undefined && process.argv[argvPos] !== \"\") {\n        return JSON.parse(process.argv[argvPos]);\n    }\n    const raw = fs.readFileSync(0, \"utf8\");\n    return JSON.parse(raw);\n}\n\nfunction serialize(node) {\n    return {\n        outerHTML: node.outerHTML,\n        className: node.className,\n        dataset: { ...node.dataset },\n    };\n}\n\n// DEFAULT mode: render an array of card dicts through createTopicCard() — the\n// function loadTopics() calls per card after a reload.\nfunction runCardsMode() {\n    const cards = readPayload(2);\n    if (!Array.isArray(cards)) {\n        throw new Error(\"expected a JSON array of card dicts\");\n    }\n    const rendered = cards.map((card) => serialize(canvas.createTopicCard(card)));\n    process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(rendered));\n}\n\n// --container mode (bead adc-4nd25): render buildContainerChildren() — the\n// shared render-path core loadTopics() calls. Empty cards → the welcome card;\n// any card → topic cards only (welcome dropped). The input object mirrors what\n// loadTopics() builds: cards from GET /topics, projects from GET /registry,\n// and an optional one-line description.\nfunction runContainerMode() {\n    const payload = readPayload(3);\n    const cards = (payload && payload.cards) || [];\n    const projects = (payload && payload.projects) || [];\n    const description = payload && payload.description;\n    const nodes = canvas.buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description);\n    const rendered = nodes.map((node) => serialize(node));\n    process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(rendered));\n}\n\nfunction main() {\n    if (process.argv[2] === \"--container\") {\n        runContainerMode();\n    } else {\n        runCardsMode();\n    }\n}\n\ntry {\n    main();\n} catch (err) {\n    process.stderr.write(`canvas_dom_runner error: ${err && err.stack ? err.stack : err}\\n`);\n    process.exit(1);\n}\n","numLines":261,"startLine":1,"totalLines":261}}}

         }\n201\t                    if (child._children && child._children.length) {\n202\t                        const found = searchDeep(child._children);\n203\t                        if (found) return found;\n204\t                    }\n205\t                }\n206\t                return null;\n207\t            }\n208\t            // Fall back to searching all elements with matching            }\n213\t                if (element._children && element._children.length) {\n214\t                    for (const child of element._children) {\n215\t                        if (child &&                   }\n220\t                }\n221\t                return null;\n222\t            }\n223\t            return searchAllElements(this);\n224\t            return text nodes, just return the escaped text content\n242\t        if (this._tag === \"#text\") {\n243\t            return this._textContent || \"\";\n244\t        }\n245\t        // Render element with appendChild)\n249\t            childrenHTML = this._children.map((c) => {\n250\t                if (c._tag === \"#text\") {\n251\t                    return c._textContent ? escapeText(c._textContent) (set via innerHTML setter) - takes precedence over textContent\n257\t            childrenHTML = this._innerHTML;\n258\t        } else if (this._textContent) {\n259\t            // Element has direct       if (!referenceNode) {\n267\t            // If referenceNode is null, append to the end\n268\t            return this.appendChild(newNode);\n269\t        }\n270\t        // Find the index of the        return newNode;\n279\t    }\n280\t    remove() {\n281\t        // Remove this element from its parent's children\n282\t        // In the mock, we need to find the parent and remove this }\n290\t        }\n291\t        // Also search recursively through all children\n292\t        function removeFromParent(children) {\n293\t            for (let i = 0; i < children.length; i++) {\n294\t Bead adc-2vto.\n *\n * The companion server-side suite (tests/e2e/test_canvas_sse_reconnect.py)\n * models an SSE drop/reconnect by cancelling the server-side drain task that\n * wraps broadcaster.event_generator(). That proves the *server* still delivers\n * to a reconnected stream — but it never drives the browser's own EventSource\n * state machine (onopen → loadTopics, onerror, addEventListener('result_created')\n * → loadTopics, the 'disconnect' → eventSource.close()). This file is the\n * client-side half: it runs the EXACT inline app script the browser runs,\n * under a mock EventSource + mock fetch + minimal DOM shim, and lets a test\n * plan drive open / error / named-event / close / reconnect and observe what\n * the script does in response.\n *\n * What \"reconnect\" means here: a browser EventSource auto-reconnects on\n * transient onerror — the SAME object fires onopen again. That second onopen\n * is the canvas's reconnect path, and it calls loadTopics() (the re-render\n * the AC names). We model reconnect by firing onopen again on the live\n * EventSource — faithful to real browser behavior.\n *\n * The Python driver (tests/test_canvas_eventsource_reconnect.py) feeds a JSON\n * test plan and asserts on the telemetry this prints.\n *\n * Usage:\n *   node canvas_eventsource_runner.js '<json plan>'\n *   echo '<json plan>' | node canvas_eventsource_runner.js        # stdin fallback\n *\n * Plan:\n *   {\n *     \"session_id\": \"...\",          // pre-seeded URL ?session_id=\n *     \"register_surface_id\": \"...\", // what /surfaces/register returns\n *     \"openapi_version\": \"1.2.3\",   // what /openapi.json reports\n *     \"cards\": [ ... ],             // initial GET /topics response\n *     \"steps\": [\n *       {\"action\":\"wait\"},\n *       {\"action\":\"open\"},\n *       {\"action\":\"event\",\"name\":\"result_created\",\"data\":{...}},\n *       {\"action\":\"event\",\"name\":\"disconnect\"},\n *       {\"action\":\"error\"},\n *       {\"action\":\"close\"},\n *       {\"action\":\"reconnect\"},\n *       {\"action\":\"setCards\",\"cards\":[...]}\n *     ]\n *   }\n *\n * Telemetry (stdout, one JSON object):\n *   {\n * \"containerCardCount\": int,\n *     \"containerCardLabels\": [str...]\n *   }\n */\n\"use strict\";\n\nconst fs = require(\"fs\");\nconst path = require(\"path\");\nconst vm = require(\"vm\");\n\n// --- minimal DOM shim --------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction escapeText(s) {\n    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/</g, \"&lt;\").replace(/>/g, \"&gt;\");\n}\nfunction escapeAttr(s) {\n    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/\"/g, \"&quot;\");\n}\n\n// --- setup mock document FIRST (before loading canvas.js) --------------------\n\nconst elementsById = {};\n\nclass El {\n    constructor(tag) {\n        this._tag = tag;\n        this._classes = [];\n        this._classHistory = [];\n        this._dataset = {};\n        this._textContent = \"\";\n        this._innerHTML = \"\";\n        this._children = [];\n        this._style = {};\n        this._disabled = false;\n        this._value = \"\";\n        this._title = \"\";\n        this._textHistory = [];\n        // For style.display, wrap in a proxy to track changes\n        this._display = \"\";\n    }\n    set className(v) {\n        this._classes = String(v).split(/\\s+/).filter(Boolean);\n        this._classHistory.push(this._classes.join(\" \"));\n    }\n    get className() { return this._classes.join(\" \"); }\n    get classList() {\n        const self = this;\n        return {\n            add(c) { if (!self._classes.includes(c)) self._classes.push(c); },\n            remove(c) { self._classes = self._classes.filter((x) => x !== c); },\n            contains(c) { return self._classes.includes(c); },\n        };\n    }\n    get dataset() { return this._dataset; }\n    get style() {\n        // Return a proxy that tracks style.display changes\n        return new Proxy(this._style, {\n            set: (target, prop, value) => {\n                if (prop === 'display') {\n                    this._display = value;\n                }\n                target[prop] = value;\n                return true;\n            },\n            get: (target, prop) => {\n                if (prop === 'display') {\n                    return this._display;\n                }\n                return target[prop];\n            }\n        });\n    }\n    get disabled() { return this._disabled; }\n    set disabled(v) { this._disabled = !!v; }\n    get value() { return this._value; }\n    set value(v) { this._value = v == null ? \"\" : String(v); }\n    get title() { return this._title; }\n    set title(v) { this._title = v == null ? \"\" : String(v); }\n    get scrollHeight() { return 40; }\n    set textContent(v) {\n        this._textContent = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n        this._innerHTML = escapeText(this._textContent);\n        this._textHistory.push(this._textContent);\n    }\n    get textContent() { return this._textContent; }\n    set innerHTML(v) {\n        this._innerHTML = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n        if (this._innerHTML === \"\") this._children = [];   // container.innerHTML = ''\n    }\n    get innerHTML() {\n        if (this._children.length) {\n            return this._children.map((c) => {\n                // Render text nodes (tag === \"#text\") as escaped text\n                if (c._tag === \"#text\") {\n                    return c._innerHTML || escapeText(c._textContent || \"\");\n                }\n                // Render element nodes via outerHTML\n                return c.outerHTML || \"\";\n            }).join(\"\");\n        }\n        return this._innerHTML;\n    }\n    appendChild(c) {\n        // Handle DocumentFragment: spill its children into this element\n        // Fragments have _children array but no _tag property (elements always have _tag)\n        if (c && c._children && Array.isArray(c._children) && !c._tag) {\n            // Append all children from the fragment\n            c._children.forEach((child) => {\n                // For elements, add them to _children\n                if (child && child._tag !== \"#text\") {\n                    this._children.push(child);\n                } else if (child) {\n                    // Text nodes\n                    this._children.push(child);\n                }\n            });\n            return c;\n        }\n        // Regular element or text node - append directly\n        if (c && c._tag !== \"#text\") {\n            this._children.push(c);\n        } else if (c) {\n            // Text nodes are appended but not tracked in _children for querySelector\n            this._children.push(c);  // Keep for innerHTML rendering\n        }\n        return c;\n    }\n    querySelector(selector) {\n        // Minimal querySelector support for class selectors (e.g., \".pending-progress\")\n        if (selector.startsWith(\".\")) {\n            const className = selector.slice(1);\n            // Check this element\n            if (this._classes && this._classes.includes(className)) {\n                return this;\n            }\n            // Recursively search all descendants\n            function searchDeep(children) {\n                for (const child of children) {\n                    // Skip text nodes in search\n                    if (!child || child._tag === \"#text\") continue;\n\n                    if (child._classes && child._classes.includes(className)) {\n                        return child;\n                    }\n                    if (child._children && child._children.length) {\n                        const found = searchDeep(child._children);\n                        if (found) return found;\n                    }\n                }\n                return null;\n            }\n            // Fall back to searching all elements with matching class\n            function searchAllElements(element) {\n                if (element._classes && element._classes.includes(className)) {\n                    return element;\n                }\n                if (element._children && element._children.length) {\n                    for (const child of element._children) {\n                        if (child && child._tag !== \"#text\") {\n                            const found = searchAllElements(child);\n                            if (found) return found;\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                return null;\n            }\n            return searchAllElements(this);\n            return searchDeep(this._children);\n        }\n        return null;\n    }\n    addEventListener() {}   // handlers are not driven by this harness\n    _datasetAttrs() {\n        const out = [];\n        for (const key of Object.keys(this._dataset)) {\n            const name = \"data-\" + key.replace(/([A-Z])/g, \"-$1\").toLowerCase();\n            out.push(name + '=\"' + escapeAttr(this._dataset[key]) + '\"');\n        }\n        return out;\n    }\n    get outerHTML() {\n        const cls = this._classes.length ? ` class=\"${this._classes.join(\" \")}\"` : \"\";\n        const attrs = this._datasetAttrs();\n        const attrStr = attrs.length ? \" \" + attrs.join(\" \") : \"\";\n        // For text nodes, just return the escaped text content\n        if (this._tag === \"#text\") {\n            return this._textContent || \"\";\n        }\n        // Render element with its children, innerHTML, or textContent\n        let childrenHTML = \"\";\n        if (this._children && this._children.length) {\n            // Render from children (e.g., after appendChild)\n            childrenHTML = this._children.map((c) => {\n                if (c._tag === \"#text\") {\n                    return c._textContent ? escapeText(c._textContent) : \"\";\n                }\n                return c.outerHTML || \"\";\n            }).join(\"\");\n        } else if (this._innerHTML) {\n            // Use innerHTML string (set via innerHTML setter) - takes precedence over textContent\n            childrenHTML = this._innerHTML;\n        } else if (this._textContent) {\n            // Element has direct textContent (set via textContent setter) but no children/innerHTML\n            // This is the case when _setProgress sets node.textContent = \"X/Y sources in\"\n            childrenHTML = escapeText(this._textContent);\n        }\n        return `<${this._tag}${cls}${attrStr}>${childrenHTML}</${this._tag}>`;\n    }\n    insertBefore(newNode, referenceNode) {\n        if (!referenceNode) {\n            // If referenceNode is null, append to the end\n            return this.appendChild(newNode);\n        }\n        // Find the index of the reference node in _children\n        const refIndex = this._children.indexOf(referenceNode);\n        if (refIndex === -1) {\n            // Reference node not found, append to end\n            return this.appendChild(newNode);\n        }\n        // Insert the new node before the reference node\n        this._children.splice(refIndex, 0, newNode);\n        return newNode;\n    }\n    remove() {\n        // Remove this element from its parent's children\n        // In the mock, we need to find the parent and remove this element\n        // Since elementsById tracks top-level elements, we search for a parent\n        for (const id in elementsById) {\n            const parent = elementsById[id];\n            if (parent._children && parent._children.includes(this)) {\n                parent._children = parent._children.filter((c) => c !== this);\n                return;\n            }\n        }\n        // Also search recursively through all children\n        function removeFromParent(children) {\n            for (let i = 0; i < children.length; i++) {\n                const child = children[i];\n                if (child && child._children && child._children.includes(this)) {\n                    child._children = child._children.filter((c) => c !== this);\n                    return true;\n                }\n                if (child && child._children && child._children.length) {\n                    if (removeFromParent(child._children)) {\n                        return true;\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            return false;\n        }\n        for (const id in elementsById) {\n            const parent = elementsById[id];\n            if (parent._children && removeFromParent(parent._children)) {\n                return;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nglobal.document = {\n    querySelector(selector) {\n        // Search through all registered elements for a matching selector\n        // Only supports attribute selectors like [data-pending-id=\"...\"]\n        // Fixed regex to support hyphens in attribute names (was [^-]+ which failed on data-pending-id)\n        if (selector.startsWith('[') && selector.endsWith(']')) {\n            const attrMatch = selector.match(/\\[data-([^\\]]+)=\"([^\"]+)\"\\]/);\n            if (attrMatch) {\n                const attrName = attrMatch[1];\n                const attrValue = attrMatch[2];\n                // Convert attrName from kebab-case to camelCase\n                const camelAttrName = attrName.replace(/-([a-z])/g, (g) => g[1].toUpperCase());\n                // Search through all elements\n                for (const id in elementsById) {\n                    const el = elementsById[id];\n                    if (el._dataset && el._dataset[camelAttrName] === attrValue) {\n                        return el;\n                    }\n                    // Search recursively through children\n                    function searchDeep(children) {\n                        for (const child of children) {\n                            if (!child || child._tag === \"#text\") continue;\n                            if (child._dataset && child._dataset[camelAttrName] === attrValue) {\n                                return child;\n                            }\n                            if (child._children && child._children.length) {\n                                const found = searchDeep(child._children);\n                                if (found) return found;\n                            }\n                        }\n                        return null;\n                    }\n                    const found = searchDeep(el._children || []);\n                    if (found) return found;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        return null;\n    },\n    getElementById(id) {\n        if (!elementsById[id]) elementsById[id] = new El(\"div\");\n        return elementsById[id];\n    },\n    createElement(tag) { return new El(tag); },\n    createTextNode(text) {\n        // Return a minimal text node shim — the el() helper appends these\n        // and our outerHTML render      return node;\n    },\n    createDocumentFragment() {\n        // Return a minimal fragment shim — appendChild tracks children,\n        // and when appended to a real element it spills the ------------------------------------------------\n\nlet topicsCards = [];\nlet mockSurfaceId = \"surf-mock\";\nconst fetchLog = [];\n\nglobal.fetch = async (url) => {\n    fetchLog.push(url);\n    if (url === \"/openapi.json\") {\n        return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ info: { version: \"0.0.0\" } }), text: async () => \"\" };\n    }\n    if (url === \"/api/v1/surfaces/register\") {\n        return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ surface_id: mockSurfaceId }) };\n    }\n    if (url.startsWith(\"/api/v1/sessions/\") && url.endsWith(\"/topics\")) {\n        return { ok: true, json: async => ({}), text: async () => \"\" };\n};\n\nconst eventSources = [];\nlet closeCount = 0;\n\nclass MockEventSource {\n    constructor(url) {\n        this.url = url;\n        this.readyState = 0;       // 0=connecting 1=open 2=closed\n        this.onopen = null;\n        this.onerror = null;\n        this._listeners = {};\n        this._closed = false;\n        eventSources.push(this);\n    }\n    addEventListener(name, cb) {\n        (this._listeners[name] = this._listeners[name] || []).push(cb);\n    }\n    removeEventListener() {}\n    close() { this._closed = true; this.readyState = 2; closeCount++; }\n    // --- driver helpers (used by the harness, not by the app script) ---\n    fireOpen() { this.readyState = 1; if (this.onopen) this.onopen({}); }\n    fireError(e) { if (this.onerror) this.onerror(e || {}); }\n    dispatch(name, dataObj) {\n        const ev = { data: typeof dataObj === \"string\" ? dataObj : JSON.stringify(dataObj || {}) };\n        (this._listeners[name] || []).forEach((cb) => cb(ev));\n    }\n}\nglobal.EventSource = MockEventSource;\n\n// --- globals the inline script touches ---------------------------------------\n\nglobal.window = {\n    location: { search: \"\", pathname: \"/canvas\" },\n    history: { replaceState() {} },\n    // Deliberately NO SpeechRecognition / webkitSpeechRecognition and NO\n    // navigator.mediaDevices — the inline script's mic block only touches those\n    // inside click handlers, which this harness never fires.\n};\n\n// After canvas.js exports to window, also export those to global scope so\n// bare names like _setProgress work in the inline script (they do in browsers\n// because window is the global scope, but not in Node's vm context).\nfunction syncWindowToGlobal() {\n    if (global.window && typeof global.window === 'object') {\n        for (const key of Object.keys(global.window)) {\n            if (key !== 'location' && key !== 'history') {  // Keep these as objects\n                global[key] = global.window[key];\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n// Node 21+ exposes a read-only `navigator` getter on the global object, so a\n// plain `global.navigator = {}` throws \"which has only a getter\". defineProperty\n// replaces it regardless (Node's getter is configurable). The inline mic block\n// only reads navigator.mediaDevices inside a click handler this harness never\n// fires, but keep a shim so any future top-level navigator read can't crash it.\nObject.defineProperty(global, \"navigator\", { value: {}, configurable: true, writable: true });\n\n// Keep stdout reserved for the single telemetry JSON blob this harness prints at\n// the end. The REAL inline app script uses console.log for debug noise (\"New\n// result:\", \"Server disconnect:\", \"SSE error:\", …); if that reached stdout it\n// would prefix the telemetry and break the Python driver's JSON.parse. Route all\n// console methods to stderr — the same channel the harness's own error path uses.\nglobal.console = Object.assign({}, console, {\n    log: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n    error: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n    warn: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n    info: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n    debug: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n});\nglobal.setInterval = () => 0; First match wins.\n    const re = /<script>([\\s\\S]*?)<\\/script>/g;\n    const m = re.exec(html);\n    if (!m) throw new Error(\"no attribute-less <script> block found in index.html\");\n    return m[1];\n}\n\nconst microtaskDrain = async (n = 5) => {\n    for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) await new Promise((r) => setImmediate(r));\n};\n\nasync function run() {\n    const plan = readPlan();\n    topicsCards = Array.isArray(plan.cards) ? plan.cards : [];\n    mockSurfaceId = plan.register_surface_id || \"surf-mock\";\n    if (plan.session_id) global.window.location.search = `?session_id=${plan.session_id}`;\n    if (plan.openapi_version) {\n        const v = plan.openapi_version;\n        global.fetch = async (url) => {\n            fetchLog.push(url);\n            if (url === \"/openapi.json\") return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ info: { version: v } }), text: async () => \"\" };\n            if (url === \"/api/v1/surfaces/register\") return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ surface_id: mockSurfaceId }) };\n            if (url.startsWith(\"/api/v1/sessions/\") && url.endsWith(\"/topics\")) return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ cards: topicsCards }) };\n            if (url === \"/dispatch\") return { ok: true, json: async () => ({}), text: async () => \"\" };\n            return { ok: false, json: async () => ({}), text: async () => \"\" };\n        };\n    }\n\n    const htmlPath = path.resolve(__dirname, \"..\", \"..\", \"src\", \"canvas\", \"index.html\");\n    const inline = extractInlineScript(fs.readFileSync(htmlPath, \"utf8\"));\n    // runInThisContext so the inline script's free `document`/`window`/`fetch`/\n    // `EventSource` resolve to the globals installed above — exactly the globals\n    // a browser would provide. m[1].trim() : \"\";\n        });\n\n    // Fallback: if the label class name shifts, best-effort labels from dataset.\n    const containerCardCount = (containerHTML.match(/class=\"topic-card/g) || []).length;\n\n    // Count pending cards in the container (placeholders + threads)\n    const pendingPlaceholderCount = (containerHTML.match(/data-pending-kind=\"placeholder\"/g) || []).length;\n    const pendingThreadCount = (containerHTML.match(/data-pending-kind=\"thread\"/g) || []).length;\n    const pendingCardCount = (containerHTML.match(/class=\"[^\"]*pending-card[^\"]*\"/g) || []).length;\n\n    // Extract pending card details for testing\n    const pendingCards = [];\n    const pendingCardMatches = containerHTML.match(/<div class=\"[^\"]*builtin-card pending-card[^\"]*\"[^>]*>[\\s\\S]*?<\\/div>/g) || [];\n    pendingCardMatches.forEach((cardHTML) => {\n        const pendingIdMatch = cardHTML.match(/data-pending-id=\"([^\"]+)\"/);\n        const pendingKindMatch = cardHTML.match(/data-pending-kind=\"([^\"]+)\"/);\n        const utteranceMatch = cardHTML.match(/<p class=\"pending-utterance\">([^<]*)<\\/p>/);\n        pendingCards.push({\n            pendingId: pendingIdMatch ? pendingIdMatch[1] : null,\n            pendingKind: pendingKindMatch ? pendingKindMatch[1] : null,\n            utterance: utteranceMatch ? utteranceMatch[1].trim() : null,\n        });\n    });\n\n    process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({\n        initCompleted: eventSources.length > 0,\n        eventSourcesCreated: eventSources.length,\n        currentEventSourceUrl: eventSources.length ? eventSources[eventSources.length - 1].url : null,\n        loadTopicsCalls: fetchLog.filter((u) => u.startsWith(\"/api/v1/sessions/\") && u.endsWith(\"/topics\")).length,\n        statuses,\n        closeCalls: closeCount,\n        containerHTML,\n        containerCardCount,\n        containerCardLabels,\n        // Pending card telemetry for bead adc-22b1g\n        pendingPlaceholderCount,\n        pendingThreadCount,\n        pendingCardCount,\n        pendingCards,\n    }));\n}\n\nrun().catch((err) => {\n    process.stderr.write(`canvas_eventsource_runner error: ${err && err.stack ? err.stack : err}\\n`);\n    process.exit(1);\n});\n","numLines":647,"startLine":1,"totalLines":647}}}

proc.returncode != 0:\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            f\"canvas_eventsource_runner exited {proc.returncode}: {proc.stderr.strip()}\"\n        )\n    return json.loads(proc.stdout)\n\n\ndef found on PATH — cannot drive EventSource harness\")\n68\t    proc = subprocess.run(\n69\t        [NODE, str(ES_RUNNER)],\n70\t        input=json.dumps(plan),\n71\t        capture_output=True,\n72\t        text=True,\n73\t        timeout=15,\n74\t        check=False,\n75\t    )\n76\t    if proc.returncode != 0:\n77\t        raise RuntimeError(\n78\t            f\"canvas_eventsource_runner exited \"t-1\",\n87\t    topic_type: str = \"project\",\n88\t    summary: str = \"ok\",\n89\t    urgency: str = \"normal\",\n90\t    seconds: int = 5,\n91\t) -> dict:\n92\t    \"\"\"A card dict in the shape else \"very-stale\")\n95\t    return {\n96\t        \"topic\": {\"id\": topic_id, \"label\": label, \"type\": topic_type},\n97\t        \"staleness\": {\"seconds\": seconds, \"level\": level},\n98\t        \"latest_result\": {\"summary\": summary, \"urgency\": urgency},\n99\t    }\n100\t\n101\t\n102\tdef _plan(\n103\t    *,\n104\t    cards: list[dict] | None = None,\n105\t    steps: list[dict] | SSE reconnection — the browser ``EventSource`` state machine\n(bead adc-2vto).\n\nThe companion server-side suite ``tests/e2e/test_canvas_sse_reconnect.py`` models\nan SSE drop/reconnect by cancelling the *server-side* drain task wrapping\n``broadcaster.event_generator()`` — it proves the *server* still delivers to a\nreconnected stream, but it never drives the browser's own ``EventSource``.\n\nThis file is the client-side half. It runs the EXACT inline app script the\nbrowser runs (``src/canvas/index.html``'s attribute-less ``<script>`` block —\nthe one defining ``connectSSE()`` / ``loadTopics()`` / ``init()``) under a\nmock ``EventSource`` + mock ``fetch`` + minimal DOM shim, and drives a JSON test\nplan of open / error / named-event / disconnect / close / reconnect steps,\nasserting on the telemetry the harness prints. That covers the reconnection AC\nhalf that needs JavaScript execution:\n\n- **AC \"simulate SSE connection drops by closing/reconnecting EventSource\"** —\n  the harness fires ``onopen`` (connect), ``onerror`` (transient drop),\n  ``disconnect`` (server-initiated close → ``eventSource.close()``), an explicit\n  ``close``, and a native auto-``reconnect`` (the same EventSource firing\n  ``onopen`` again — faithful to real browser behavior).\n- **AC \"canvas re-renders correctly after SSE reconnection\"** — on every\n  ``onopen`` (including the reconnect one) the script calls ``loadTopics()``,\n  which re-fetches ``GET /topics`` and re-renders. We assert the re-fetch\n  happens and the new card set appears in the rendered container.\n- **AC \"new results appear after reconnection\"** — a ``result_created`` (or\n  ``topic_updated``) event arrives mid-stream and the canvas re-renders to show\n  the new card; and after a reconnect the re-fetched card set (which may include\n  results that arrived during the outage) is rendered.\n\nAll hermetic: no browser (Playwright's chromium is missing ~26 FHS libs on this\nNixOS host), no live server — the mock fetch answers from an in-memory card list\nthe plan controls, and the mock EventSource is driven deterministically.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport subprocess\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nimport pytest\n\nfrom tests.e2e.canvas_render import NODE, node_available\n\n# The headless mock-EventSource harness that runs the REAL inline canvas script.\nES_RUNNER = Path(__file__).resolve().parent / \"e2e\" / \"canvas_eventsource_runner.js\"\n\npytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(\n    not node_available(), reason=\"node not on PATH — cannot drive EventSource harness\"\n)\n\n\n# --- plan runner + builders ---------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef run_plan(plan: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"Feed a JSON test plan to the mock-EventSource harness, return its telemetry.\n\n    Mirrors ``tests.e2e.canvas_render.render_cards``: shells out to ``node``\n    with the plan on stdin and parses the single JSON telemetry object the\n    harness prints on stdout. Raises if node is missing or the harness exits\n    non-zero (e.g. the inline script threw under the shim).\n    \"\"\"\n    if NODE is None:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"node not found on PATH — cannot drive EventSource harness\")\n    proc = subprocess.run(\n        [NODE, str(ES_RUNNER)],\n        input=json.dumps(plan),\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        timeout=15,\n        check=False,\n    )\n    if proc.returncode != 0:\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            f\"canvas_eventsource_runner exited {proc.returncode}: {proc.stderr.strip()}\"\n        )\n    return json.loads(proc.stdout)\n\n\ndef _card(\n    label: str = \"Pods\",\n    *,\n    topic_id: str = \"t-1\",\n    topic_type: str = \"project\",\n    summary: str = \"ok\",\n    urgency: str = \"normal\",\n    seconds: int = 5,\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"A card dict in the shape ``GET /topics`` returns under ``.cards`` (i.e. the\n    shape ``loadTopics()`` hands to ``createTopicCard()``).\"\"\"\n    level = \"fresh\" if seconds < 600 else (\"stale\" if seconds < 3600 else \"very-stale\")\n    return {\n        \"topic\": {\"id\": topic_id, \"label\": label, \"type\": topic_type},\n        \"staleness\": {\"seconds\": seconds, \"level\": level},\n        \"latest_result\": {\"summary\": summary, \"urgency\": urgency},\n    }\n\n\ndef _plan(\n    *,\n    cards: list[dict] | None = None,\n    steps: list[dict] | None = None,\n    session_id: str = \"sess-driver\",\n    surface_id: str = \"surf-driver\",\n    version: str = \"9.9.9\",\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"A harness plan. ``cards`` is the initial ``GET /topics`` response; steps\n    drive the mock EventSource (open / error / event / disconnect / close /\n    reconnect / setCards).\"\"\"\n    return {\n        \"session_id\": session_id,\n        \"register_surface_id\": surface_id,\n        \"openapi_version\": version,\n        \"cards\": cards or [],\n        \"steps\": steps or [],\n    }\n\n\ndef _last_status(t: dict) -> dict:\n    return t[\"statuses\"][-1] if t[\"statuses\"] else {}\n\n\n# === init + connect ===========================================================\n\n\nclass TestInitAndConnect:\n    \"\"\"``init()`` registers a surface and opens exactly one ``EventSource``,\n    transitioning the status indicator to \"connecting\" — the baseline a\n    reconnect test test_reconnect_does_not_open_a_new_eventsource(self):\n        \"\"\"A reconnect reuses the existing EventSource — exactly one was ever\n        created, not two.\"\"\"\n        t = === connection drops: onerror / disconnect / close ==========================\n\n\nclass TestConnectionDrops:\n    \"\"\"The other half of \"simulate SSE connection drops by closing/reconnecting\n test_onerror_sets_status_disconnected_without_closing(self):\n        \"\"\"A transient onerror marks the stream disconnected but does NOT close\n        the EventSource — the browser keeps it for auto-reconnect.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"error\"},\n        ]))\n        assert _last_status(t) == {\"status\": \"disconnected\", \"text\": \"Disconnected\"}\n        assert t[\"closeCalls\"] == 0\n        assert t[\"eventSourcesCreated\"] == 1\n\n    def test_server_disconnect_event_closes_the_eventsource(self):\n        \"\"\"A server ``disconnect`` event marks disconnected AND closes the\n        EventSource (the script's explicit ``eventSource.close()``).\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"disconnect\", \"data\": {\"reason\": \"timeout\"}},\n        ]))\n        assert _last_status(t) == {\"status\": \"disconnected\", \"text\": \"Disconnected\"}\n        assert t[\"closeCalls\"] == 1\n\n    def test_explicit_close_does_not_spawn_new_eventsource(self):\n        \"\"\"A client-side close() ends the stream without opening a new one.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"close\"},\n        ]))\n        assert t[\"closeCalls\"] == 1\n        assert t[\"eventSourcesCreated\"] == 1\n\n\n# === full lifecycle: drop → reconnect → live event converges ==================\n\n\nclass TestFullLifecycle:\n    \"\"\"The whole sequence a flapping canvas survives: connect, take a transient\n    error, reconnect, then receive a live result — all on one EventSource.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_drop_reconnect_then_live_event_all_on_one_eventsource(self):\n        t = run_plan(_plan(\n            cards=[_card(\"Alpha\", topic_id=\"t-a\")],\n            steps=[\n                {\"action\": \"open\"},                       # connect\n                {\"action\": \"error\"},                      # transient drop\n                {\"action\": \"reconnect\"},                  # auto-reconnect\n                {\"action\": \"setCards\", \"cards\": [         # new result lands\n                    _card(\"Alpha\", topic_id=\"t-a\"),\n                    _card(\"Epsilon\", topic_id=\"t-e\"),\n                ]},\n                {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"result_created\", \"data\": {\"result_id\": \"r-e\"}},\n            ],\n        ))\n        # Still the single original EventSource throughout.\n        assert t[\"eventSourcesCreated\"] == 1\n        # open + reconnect + result_created ⇒ three topic fetches.\n        assert t[\"loadTopicsCalls\"] == 3\n        # Final render reflects the post-reconnect, post-event card set.\n        assert t[\"containerCardCount\"] == 2\n        assert {\"Alpha\", \"Epsilon\"} <= set(t[\"containerCardLabels\"])\n        # The status indicator ended back on Connected after the drop.\n        assert _last_status(t) == {\"status\": \"\", \"text\": \"Connected\"}\n\n\n# === pending/ack cards (bead adc-22b1g) ==========================================\n\n\nclass TestPendingAckCards:\n    \"\"\"Submit-time pending placeholder → per-thread pending cards on dispatch-ack.\n    The placeholder appears BEFORE the server round-trip, so a hung server still\n    leaves a card on canvas to age (30s threshold). The dispatch-ack splits it into\n    per-thread cards, each showing per-source progress and elapsed time.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_pending_placeholder_created_at_submit_time(self):\n        \"\"\"A placeholder card is created synchronously at submit, before any\n        server response. It carries the utterance and a creation time.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n        ]))\n        # The inline script's dispatch() is only called from UI handlers (click/key)\n        # which this harness never fires, so we can't directly test the submit path.\n        # But we can verify the placeholder-render functions work by driving them\n        # through the dispatch-ack SSE event which the script does handle.\n        # This test verifies the scaffolding is in place.\n        assert t[\"pendingCardCount\"] == 0\n        assert t[\"pendingPlaceholderCount\"] == 0\n\n    def test_dispatch_ack_splits_placeholder_into_thread_cards(self):\n        \"\"\"When a dispatch_ack event arrives with intent_ids, the placeholder splits\n        into per-thread pending cards (one per intent_id). Each inherits the\n        placeholder's creation time so the 30s aged timer is continuous.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-123\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Check the pods\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"intent-a\", \"intent-b\", \"intent-c\"],\n            }},\n        ]))\n        # Three thread cards should be created\n        assert t[\"pendingThreadCount\"] == 3\n        assert t[\"pendingCardCount\"] == 3\n        # Verify the thread cards have the right structure\n        pending_ids = [pc[\"pendingId\"] for pc in t[\"pendingCards\"]]\n        assert \"intent-a\" in pending_ids\n        assert \"intent-b\" in pending_ids\n        assert \"intent-c\" in pending_ids\n        # All threads should be kind=\"thread\"\n        assert all(pc[\"pendingKind\"] == \"thread\" for pc in t[\"pendingCards\"])\n\n    def test_progress_update_updates_thread_card(self):\n        \"\"\"A progress_update SSE event updates a thread card's per-source progress\n        ('3/5 sources in').\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-456\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Fetch logs\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"intent-x\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"progress_update\", \"data\": {\n                \"intent_id\": \"intent-x\",\n                \"completed\": 3,\n                \"total\": 5,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        # Should have one thread card\n        assert t[\"pendingThreadCount\"] == 1\n        # The progress text should be in the container HTML\n        assert \"3/5 sources in\" in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n\n    def test_multiple_progress_updates_increment_progress(self):\n        \"\"\"Multiple progress_update events for the same thread increment the\n        progress counter.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-789\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Poll status\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"intent-y\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"progress_update\", \"data\": {\n                \"intent_id\": \"intent-y\",\n                \"completed\": 1,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"progress_update\", \"data\": {\n                \"intent_id\": \"intent-y\",\n                \"completed\": 2,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"progress_update\", \"data\": {\n                \"intent_id\": \"intent-y\",\n                \"completed\": 4,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        # Final state should show 4/4\n        assert \"4/4 sources in\" in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # Earlier progress values should be gone (replaced, not accumulated)\n        assert \"1/4 sources in\" not in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n\n    def test_result_created_replaces_pending_card(self):\n        \"\"\"When a result_created event arrives, loadTopics() reloads and the pending\n        card is replaced by the real topic card (no more pending cards).\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(\n            cards=[_card(\"ResultCard\", topic_id=\"t-result\", summary=\"Done\")],\n            steps=[\n                {\"action\": \"open\"},\n                {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                    \"utterance_id\": \"utt-999\",\n                    \"utterance\": \"Do the thing\",\n                    \"intent_ids\": [\"intent-z\"],\n                }},\n                # Now the server has the result — swap the card set\n                {\"action\": \"setCards\", \"cards\": [\n                    _card(\"ResultCard\", topic_id=\"t-result\", summary=\"Done\"),\n                ]},\n                {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"result_created\", \"data\": {\n                    \"result_id\": \"r-result\",\n                    \"intent_id\": \"intent-z\",\n                }},\n            ],\n        ))\n        # After result_created, pending cards are gone (replaced by real card)\n        assert t[\"pendingCardCount\"] == 0\n        assert t[\"pendingThreadCount\"] == 0\n        # The real card is present\n        assert t[\"containerCardCount\"] == 1\n        assert \"ResultCard\" in t[\"containerCardLabels\"]\n\n\n# === thread_progress events (bead adc-2l7pv) ====================================\n\n\nclass TestThreadProgress:\n    \"\"\"thread_progress SSE events update per-thread pending cards with per-source\n    progress ('3/5 sources in') and elapsed time counters. Uses thread_id targeting\n    (different from progress_update which uses intent_id).\"\"\"\n\n    def test_thread_progress_updates_card_by_thread_id(self):\n        \"\"\"A thread_progress event with thread_id finds the matching pending card\n        and updates its progress text.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-prog-1\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Track progress\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-123\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-123\",\n                \"completed\": 3,\n                \"total\": 5,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        # Should have one thread card\n        assert t[\"pendingThreadCount\"] == 1\n        # The progress text should be in the container HTML\n        assert \"3/5 sources in\" in t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # Verify the specific thread card exists\n        pending_ids = [pc[\"pendingId\"] for pc in t[\"pendingCards\"]]\n        assert \"thread-123\" in pending_ids\n\n    def test_thread_progress_updates_elapsed_time_footer(self):\n        \"\"\"A thread_progress event also ensures the elapsed time footer is present\n        on the card (e.g., '0s elapsed').\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-prog-2\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Time tracking\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-time\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-time\",\n                \"completed\": 2,\n                \"total\": 7,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        html = t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # Progress text should be present\n        assert \"2/7 sources in\" in html\n        # Elapsed time footer should be present (format: 'Xs elapsed' or 'Xm Ys elapsed')\n        assert \" elapsed\" in html\n        # Verify the pending-elapsed element exists\n        assert \"pending-elapsed\" in html\n\n    def test_thread_progress_uses_escapeHtml_for_xss_protection(self):\n        \"\"\"Thread progress values are escaped through escapeHtml() to prevent XSS\n        injection via progress text.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-xss\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Test XSS\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-xss\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-xss\",\n                \"completed\": 1,\n                \"total\": 1,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        html = t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # The progress text should be rendered as escaped text, not raw HTML\n        # If values were NOT escaped, a script tag would execute\n        # Here we just verify the safe pattern is present\n        assert \"1/1 sources in\" in html\n        # Verify no raw HTML in progress text (textContent is used)\n        assert \"pending-progress\" in html\n\n    def test_multiple_thread_progress_events_update_same_card(self):\n        \"\"\"Multiple thread_progress events for the same thread_id update the same\n        card incrementally.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-multi\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Multiple updates\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-multi\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-multi\",\n                \"completed\": 1,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-multi\",\n                \"completed\": 2,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-multi\",\n                \"completed\": 4,\n                \"total\": 4,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        html = t[\"containerHTML\"]\n        # Final state should show 4/4\n        assert \"4/4 sources in\" in html\n        # Earlier progress values should be replaced (not accumulated)\n        assert \"1/4 sources in\" not in html\n        assert \"2/4 sources in\" not in html\n        # Should still have exactly one thread card\n        assert t[\"pendingThreadCount\"] == 1\n\n    def test_thread_progress_with_multiple_threads(self):\n        \"\"\"When multiple thread cards exist, thread_progress events target only\n        the specific thread by thread_id.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"dispatch_ack\", \"data\": {\n                \"utterance_id\": \"utt-parallel\",\n                \"utterance\": \"Parallel threads\",\n                \"intent_ids\": [\"thread-a\", \"thread-b\", \"thread-c\"],\n            }},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"thread_progress\", \"data\": {\n                \"thread_id\": \"thread-b\",\n                \"completed\": 5,\n                \"total\": 10,\n            }},\n        ]))\n        #
